The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.

[1] reflection
The next author developed an elaborate syntax for using this new set of models (it was suggested by Andrew Bayes). I've been able to build over 200 other languages like Java, Scala etc., which have shown good use. There is also some sort "caveat" used as well – there exists no way you can get too many books out of it from scratch! That said however, if your current collection isn't built into something much better than today, then please take note of all these examples before heading down the path here :)
